2604.26779.title: Accelerating RL Post-Training Rollouts via System-Integrated Speculative Decoding
2604.26779.paper_summary: RL post-training of frontier language models is increasingly bottlenecked by autoregressive rollout generation, making rollout acceleration a central systems challenge. Many existing efficiency methods improve throughput by changing the rollout or optimization regime, for example, through off-policy execution, replay, or lower-precision generation. We study speculative decoding as a lossless acceleration primitive for RL rollouts that preserves the target model's output distribution. We implement speculative decoding in NeMo-RL with a vLLM backend, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous pipelines and enabling speculation during RL rollouts. This benefit is realizable across speculation mechanisms, such as pretrained MTP heads, small external draft models or even techniques such as Eagle3, which are traditionally applied after RL phase. This yields a deployment path for state-of-the-art speculative decoding inside RL training. In a reasoning post-training workload at 8B scale under synchronous RL, speculative decoding improves rollout throughput by 1.8x. Using a high-fidelity performance simulator, we project that combining speculative decoding with asynchronous RL yields up to 2.5x end-to-end training speedup at 235B scale.
2604.26779.paper_summary_zh: RL後訓練的前沿語言模型越來越受到自回歸展開生成的瓶頸限制，使得展開加速成為一個核心系統挑戰。許多現有的效率方法通過改變展開或優化機制來提高吞吐量，例如，通過非策略執行、重放或低精度生成。我們研究了推測解碼作為一種無損加速原語，用於保持目標模型輸出分佈的RL展開。我們在NeMo-RL中實現了推測解碼，使用vLLM後端，支持同步和異步管道，並在RL展開期間啟用推測。這一好處可以通過各種推測機制實現，例如預訓練的MTP頭、小型外部草稿模型，甚至是傳統上在RL階段後應用的技術，如Eagle3。這為在RL訓練中內部實現最先進的推測解碼提供了一條部署路徑。在同步RL下的8B規模推理後訓練工作負載中，推測解碼將展開吞吐量提高了1.8倍。使用高保真性能模擬器，我們預測將推測解碼與異步RL結合可在235B規模下實現高達2.5倍的端到端訓練加速。
2604.26779.author: Hayate Iso et.al.
2604.26779.authors: Hayate Iso, Tiyasa Mitra, Sudipta Mondal, Rasoul Shafipour, Venmugil Elango, Terry Kong, Yuki Huang, Seonjin Na, Izzy Putterman, Benjamin Chislett, Maor Ashkenazi, Joseph Guman, Gerald Shen, Tugrul Konuk, Ashwath Aithal, Ritika Borkar, Ran Zilberstein, Bita Rouhani
